# Error Handling Checklist: Optional gh-stack stack manager integration

**Purpose**: Validate error-handling requirement quality for fallback boundaries, failed `gh stack` command classification, recoverable block state, and duplicate-safe retry behavior.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all pre-mutation fallback causes documented, including missing, unsupported, ambiguous, read-only-proof-failed, topology-incompatible, unsafe, and stack-manager planning failures, while invalid packet validation is a hard block before either manager mutates? [Verified, Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Error classification rules, Quickstart §2-4, Tasks T031/T036/T038]
- [x] CHK002 Are all no-fallback mutation boundaries defined, including local branch creation/update, remote push, PR create/edit/sync, rebase/restack, and stack metadata writes? [Verified, Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Data Model §Command Plan, Tasks T031/T032/T046-T047]
- [x] CHK003 Does the recoverable block contract require failed mutating command evidence, side-effect classification, mutation boundary, pre/post-failure topology, prior successful PRs, resume boundary, retry policy, and evidence paths? [Resolved, Spec §FR-012, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §Recoverable Block State, Contract §recovery]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK004 Are failed `gh stack` command classifications defined clearly enough to distinguish no side effects, successful planned mutation, observed partial mutation, unknown side effects, and same-manager no-change reconciliation? [Resolved, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Error classification rules, Data Model §Command Execution Evidence]
- [x] CHK005 Is fallback after read-only detection or planning failure clearly distinguished from forbidden fallback after an attempted topology-changing `gh stack` command? [Verified, Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Error classification rules, Tasks T032/T038/T047/T053]
- [x] CHK006 Are duplicate-safe retry requirements specific about matching slice ID, head branch, base branch, PR number or URL, head SHA, and current validated PR packet before any create or sync? [Verified, Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Quickstart §6, Tasks T030/T033/T039]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK007 Does the shared schema enforce that blocked decisions carry `fallback_allowed=false` with a partial or unknown mutation-boundary status, rather than allowing an under-specified blocked state? [Resolved, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013, Contract §stack-manager-decision]
- [x] CHK008 Do spec, data model, quickstart, and schema use the same blocked-flow terms: `blocked`, `partial_mutation`, `partial_mutation_unknown`, and `fallback_allowed=false`? [Verified, Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Data Model §Command Execution Evidence, Contract §mutation_boundary, Tasks T032/T047]
- [x] CHK009 Are emission link/sync failures and restack rebase/sync failures governed by the same no-manager-mixing and recoverable-block requirements? [Verified, Consistency, Spec §FR-011, Plan §Phase 1, Tasks T032/T038/T047/T053]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK010 Are validation scenarios required for clean pre-mutation failure, ambiguous mutating `link_stack`, mutating `sync_stack`, mutating `rebase_upstack`, and same-manager reconciliation after no topology change? [Resolved, Spec §FR-017, Quickstart §5, Quickstart §7]
- [x] CHK011 Are retry scenarios required to prove existing matching PRs are reused and mismatched or ambiguous topology blocks before duplicate PR creation? [Verified, Coverage, Spec §FR-014, Quickstart §6, Tasks T030/T033/T039/T045/T048]
- [x] CHK012 Are reviewer-facing failure evidence requirements measurable enough to prove a blocked run can be diagnosed without rerunning or switching managers? [Verified, Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-005, Data Model §Recoverable Block State, Tasks T049/T055/T057-T060]

## Notes

- Resolved CHK003 and CHK007 by tightening `contracts/stack-manager-decision.schema.json` so blocked decisions require structured recovery evidence, failed `gh-stack` execution evidence, partial/unknown mutation boundary status, and `fallback_allowed=false`.
- Resolved CHK004 by adding explicit side-effect classification rules to `data-model.md` and mirroring them in `plan.md`.
- Resolved CHK010 by expanding `quickstart.md` failure fixtures for clean pre-mutation failures, mutating `link_stack`, `sync_stack`, `rebase_upstack`, and same-manager no-change reconciliation.
- Analyze clarified that PRSG-012 packet-validation failures are pre-mutation hard blocks, not stack-manager fallback causes, and verified the remaining error-handling checklist items against generated tasks.

## Verification Rerun

- [x] CHK013 Does the recoverable block schema now require failed `gh-stack` execution evidence with side-effect class, mutation boundary, topology evidence, prior PRs, resume boundary, retry policy, and evidence paths? [Resolved Verification, Contract §recovery]
- [x] CHK014 Are failed-command classification rules now explicit for `none`, `planned_mutation`, `partial_mutation`, `partial_mutation_unknown`, and same-manager no-change reconciliation? [Resolved Verification, Data Model §Command Execution Evidence, Plan §Error classification rules]
- [x] CHK015 Does the blocked schema now constrain `mutation_boundary.status` and failed-operation `side_effect_class` to partial or unknown mutation states with `fallback_allowed=false`? [Resolved Verification, Contract §stack-manager-decision]
- [x] CHK016 Do quickstart scenarios now require clean pre-mutation failure, mutating `link_stack`, `sync_stack`, `rebase_upstack`, duplicate retry mismatch, and same-manager reconciliation coverage? [Resolved Verification, Quickstart §5, Quickstart §6, Quickstart §7]
